name: Diarmuid Ua Duibhne
shortDescription: Diarmuid Ua Duibhne is a renowned warrior in Irish mythology, famed for his beauty and his tragic love affair with Gráinne. He is a central figure in the Fenian Cycle.
description: Diarmuid Ua Duibhne, also known as Diarmuid of the Love Spot, is a prominent warrior of the Fianna in Irish mythology, belonging to the Fenian Cycle. He is celebrated for his unparalleled beauty, a trait stemming from a magical love spot granted to him by a woman. This love spot made him irresistible to women, a characteristic that leads to his most famous tale: his elopement with Gráinne, the betrothed of the aging Fionn mac Cumhaill. Their flight across Ireland, pursued by Fionn and his warriors, is a central narrative. Diarmuid's skill as a warrior is legendary; he wields the formidable sword Móralltach and the spear Gáe Dearg. Despite his prowess and Aengus Óg's attempts to protect him, Diarmuid's fate is sealed by Fionn's jealousy and treachery. He is ultimately killed by a magical boar while hunting, a fate foretold and possibly orchestrated by Fionn. Diarmuid represents ideals of youthful beauty, chivalry, and tragic love within Celtic mythology.

sources.title: The Pursuit of Diarmuid and Gráinne
sources.author: Standish Hayes O'Grady (translator)
